problem_statement: Recipe quality and pantry fit are subjective enough that regressions will slip through if the feature launches without repeatable checks. Without fixture-based evaluation and fallback tests, prompt or model changes could quietly degrade recipe realism and trust.
in_scope: Create pantry fixtures that represent small medium and large household stock states
Add tests or scripted evaluations for schema validity pantry-fit labeling and failure handling
Document the expected quality bar and manual review checklist for future prompt or model changes
requirements: Evaluation fixtures must cover pantry-rich pantry-thin and expiring-item scenarios
The generation path must be testable without live OpenAI calls in routine CI runs
Fallback behavior must be covered when the provider is unavailable or returns invalid output
acceptance_criteria: The repo contains repeatable evaluation coverage for AI recipe generation inputs and outputs
Malformed responses and provider failures are exercised in tests or scripted checks
Future model or prompt updates have a documented quality bar to compare against
risks: Tests may become too coupled to one prompt wording if fixtures assert exact recipe text
Without a mockable abstraction the eval path may require live network access and become flaky
implementation_notes: Favor structure and pantry-fit assertions over exact wording assertions. Add a thin abstraction around the OpenAI call so tests can inject deterministic draft payloads.
